subject: Mathematics
title: Fractals: Infinite Detail
description: A micro-lesson that makes Fractals: Infinite Detail usable.

hook.text: Zoom into a fractal and you see the same pattern forever. There's no end to the detail.

buildup.text: The Koch snowflake has a finite area but an infinite perimeter—a paradox of geometry.

discovery.text: Fractals have non-integer dimensions. The Koch curve is about 1.26-dimensional—between a line and a plane.

twist.text: Mandelbrot showed that fractal geometry describes coastlines, clouds, and blood vessels better than Euclid.

climax.text: Fractals revealed that infinity hides in the geometry of everyday objects—not just abstract math.

punchline.text: Infinite complexity from a simple rule, repeated forever.

quiz.question: What is unique about the Koch snowflake?
options: It has finite area but infinite perimeter
